summary: Patrick McKenzie shares the raw statistics from building Bingo Card Creator in its first week of development. The post reveals a remarkably lean operation: 47 hours of work, 2,226 lines of Java code, and a total budget where the most expensive item was a $17.20 international fax. The brevity of the post itself mirrors the minimalist approach to launching the product.
key_points: The entire product was built in 47 hours split across programming (31), website (10), and payment processor setup (6)
The codebase was modest at 2,226 lines of Java with roughly 50 total classes
The most expensive cost was a $17.20 fax between Japan and Nebraska for a contract, highlighting the near-zero startup costs of a micro-ISV
money_quotes: Hours worked: 47 hours (programming 31, website 10, payment processors 6)
Most expensive line item on budget: $17.20. One 10 page fax of a contract from Gifu Prefecture to Nebraska.
key_insight: A viable software product can be launched with minimal investment — 47 hours of work and less than $20 in expenses — demonstrating the extraordinarily low barrier to entry for micro-ISV businesses.
